subtitle: Key milestones across three decades of child support specialisation.

items.title: Established Child Support Advisory Services
items.description: Transitioned to advisory model, providing specialist guidance through administrative channels at reduced cost.

items.title: Child Support Editor, Family Law Review (Thomson Reuters)
items.description: Appointed Child Support Editor for the Family Law Review, contributing peer-reviewed articles on substitution orders, amicus curiae appearances, pre-emptive declaratory orders, overseas child support, and the autochthonous expedient. Also published in the Law Institute Journal on depreciation and child support.

items.title: Founded Melbourne Practice
items.description: Established specialist child support practice at Normanby Chambers, 430 Little Collins Street, Melbourne. Built a national client base spanning every state and territory.

items.title: Principal Lawyer, Legal Aid Queensland — Child Support Division
items.description: Led the child support division, gaining extensive experience in assessment disputes, enforcement, and agreements for vulnerable clients.

items.title: Bachelor of Laws, University of Melbourne
items.description: Graduated and chose to specialise exclusively in child support — a field few practitioners understood at the time.
